findings.title: User Behavior Tracking Pipeline
findings.description: Implement a data ingestion pipeline that captures browsing history, click patterns, scroll depth, and time-on-page metrics for each authenticated user. The pipeline must handle at minimum 50k events/second at peak.
assumptions: Users will consent to data tracking for personalized recommendations.
recommendations: Develop a robust data ingestion architecture.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the user is authenticated, WHEN they browse content, THEN their browsing history, click patterns, and scroll depth are captured and streamed to the data lake in real-time.

findings.title: ML Recommendation Engine Implementation
findings.description: Build a recommendation engine that uses accumulated user profile data to serve personalized content suggestions, targeting a 20% improvement in content CTR.
assumptions: The recommendation engine will effectively utilize user profile data.
recommendations: Incorporate collaborative filtering and content-based approaches.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN user profile data is available, WHEN the recommendation engine processes this data, THEN it should serve personalized content suggestions with a target CTR improvement of 20% within 60 days.

findings.title: User Preference Dashboard Creation
findings.description: Build a user-facing dashboard that displays collected behavioral data, allowing users to see their inferred interests and opt out of specific tracking categories.
assumptions: Users want to understand and control their data.
recommendations: Ensure the dashboard is user-friendly and informative.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the user accesses their dashboard, WHEN they view their data, THEN they should see inferred interests and have options to opt out of specific tracking categories.

findings.title: Cross-Device Tracking Implementation
findings.description: Link user activity across mobile app and web browser sessions using deterministic and probabilistic matching to enable a unified user profile.
assumptions: Users will be logged in across devices.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user is logged in on multiple devices, WHEN they perform actions, THEN their activities should be linked to create a unified profile.

findings.title: Data Export API for Advertisers
findings.description: Create an API and batch export system that shares anonymized user profile segments with advertising partners while ensuring compliance with privacy regulations.
assumptions: Advertisers require cohort-level data for effective targeting.
recommendations: Validate the anonymization pipeline to prevent re-identification.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the data export system is implemented, WHEN advertisers request data, THEN they should receive anonymized cohort-level data without individual identifiers.

findings.title: GDPR Compliance Audit Preparation
findings.description: Conduct a full GDPR compliance audit to address concerns regarding lawful data processing, consent mechanisms, and data retention policies.
assumptions: Compliance with GDPR is essential for market launch.
recommendations: Complete the audit before product launch.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the audit team is assembled, WHEN they review the data processing activities, THEN they should identify and document all compliance gaps.

findings.title: User Feedback Collection Mechanism
findings.description: Implement a mechanism for collecting user feedback on privacy settings and data tracking transparency to inform future iterations.
assumptions: User feedback is critical for improving privacy features.
recommendations: Create surveys and feedback forms accessible from the user dashboard.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the feedback mechanism is live, WHEN users provide feedback, THEN their responses should be collected and analyzed for future improvements.

findings.title: Privacy Settings Simplification
findings.description: Redesign the privacy settings interface to simplify user interactions and enhance clarity regarding data tracking options.
assumptions: Users will benefit from a clearer privacy settings interface.
recommendations: Conduct user testing to validate the new design.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the new privacy settings interface is implemented, WHEN users access their settings, THEN they should find it easier to modify their preferences.

findings.title: Transparency in Data Tracking
findings.description: Provide clear information to users about what data is being tracked and how it is used, enhancing transparency.
assumptions: Users need to understand data usage for trust.

evidence.excerpt: Need for Transparency in Data Tracking

recommendations: Create a dedicated section in the user dashboard for data tracking information.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the transparency section is live, WHEN users access it, THEN they should see detailed information on data tracking practices.

findings.title: Granular Consent Mechanisms for GDPR
findings.description: Implement granular consent mechanisms that allow users to opt-in or opt-out of specific data processing activities to ensure GDPR compliance.
assumptions: Users require control over their data processing preferences.
recommendations: Design consent forms that clearly outline data processing activities.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the consent mechanisms are implemented, WHEN users access their settings, THEN they should see options to opt-in or opt-out of specific data processing activities.

findings.title: Monitoring and Alerting for User Engagement Metrics
findings.description: Implement monitoring and alerting systems for key user engagement metrics to ensure performance and identify issues early.
assumptions: Monitoring is essential for maintaining user engagement.

evidence.excerpt: North Star Metric: User Engagement Score

recommendations: Set up dashboards for real-time monitoring of engagement metrics.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the monitoring system is in place, WHEN user engagement metrics change, THEN alerts should be triggered for significant deviations.

findings.title: User Retention Rate Monitoring
findings.description: Establish a system to monitor user retention rates over 30 days to ensure the platform retains users after initial engagement.
assumptions: User retention is crucial for platform success.

evidence.excerpt: Guardrail Metric: User Retention Rate (30 Days)

recommendations: Implement a dashboard to visualize retention trends.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the retention monitoring system is established, WHEN users engage with the platform, THEN retention rates should be tracked and reported over a 30-day period.

summary: The analysis resulted in a comprehensive set of requirements focused on enhancing user privacy, data tracking transparency, and compliance with GDPR. Each requirement is structured with clear acceptance criteria and categorized by priority and complexity, ensuring a robust product backlog for the PersonaLens user profiling system.
